metadata.description: Rwanda NCSA cybersecurity control requirements for macOS compliance auditing - 47 system-auditable controls

RWNCSA-AC-001.name: Account Management - Login History Monitoring
RWNCSA-AC-001.description: Organizations must maintain and monitor user login history to detect unauthorized access attempts and maintain accountability

fail_conditions: Logs incomplete or missing critical fields

steps: Enable login auditing: sudo defaults write /Library/Preferences/com.apple.loginwindow LoginwindowLaunchesRelaunchApps -bool false
Configure retention: sudo defaults write /Library/Preferences/com.apple.alf loggingenabled -bool true

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Cannot detect unauthorized access, no audit trail for security incidents
risk_assessment.business_impact: Inability to investigate security breaches, compliance violation

RWNCSA-AC-002.name: Account Management - User Account Control
RWNCSA-AC-002.description: Organizations must maintain inventory of user accounts and ensure principle of least privilege

steps: Remove unauthorized admin: sudo dseditgroup -o edit -d username -t user admin

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: CRITICAL - Unauthorized access, privilege escalation possible

RWNCSA-AC-010.name: Concurrent Session Control
RWNCSA-AC-010.description: Monitor and control concurrent user sessions to prevent unauthorized access

pass_conditions: Session information includes user, terminal, start time

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Session hijacking, unauthorized access possible

RWNCSA-AU-002.name: Audit Events Logging
RWNCSA-AU-002.description: System must generate audit records for security-relevant events

pass_conditions: Logs contain timestamp, event type, user, outcome

steps: Configure retention: sudo log config --subsystem com.apple.system --category default --ttl 90

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: CRITICAL - No audit trail, cannot detect or investigate incidents
risk_assessment.business_impact: Compliance violation, inability to forensic analysis

RWNCSA-AU-004.name: Audit Storage Capacity
RWNCSA-AU-004.description: Ensure adequate storage for audit logs and prevent log loss due to space constraints

pass_conditions: Sufficient space for 90 days of logs

steps: Enable log rotation: sudo periodic daily weekly monthly
Monitor space: sudo defaults write /Library/Preferences/SystemConfiguration/com.apple.PowerManagement.plist DiskSpaceWarningLevel -int 15

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Log loss, audit trail gaps, compliance issues
risk_assessment.business_impact: Inability to investigate incidents, regulatory penalties

RWNCSA-CM-002.name: Baseline Configuration
RWNCSA-CM-002.description: Maintain baseline configuration of system components for security and compliance

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: LOW - Configuration drift, patch management issues

RWNCSA-IA-005.name: Authenticator Management - Password Policy
RWNCSA-IA-005.description: Enforce strong password requirements to prevent unauthorized access

macos_implementation.validation: min_length >= 12 AND complexity_enabled AND max_age <= 90 AND lockout_enabled

remediation.expected_result: Policy XML showing length >= 12, complexity, expiry, lockout

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: CRITICAL - Weak passwords, brute force attacks, unauthorized access
risk_assessment.business_impact: Account compromise, data breach, compliance violation

RWNCSA-SI-003.name: Malicious Code Protection - Process Monitoring
RWNCSA-SI-003.description: Monitor running processes for malicious or unauthorized software

steps: Check for malware: sudo launchctl list | grep -v com.apple

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Malware infection, data theft, system compromise
risk_assessment.business_impact: Data breach, system downtime, reputational damage

RWNCSA-SI-007.name: System Integrity Protection
RWNCSA-SI-007.description: Enable system-level protections against unauthorized modifications and malware

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: CRITICAL - System vulnerable to rootkits, kernel exploits, unsigned malware
risk_assessment.business_impact: Complete system compromise, data theft, malware infection

RWNCSA-AC-003.name: Least Privilege - Administrative Access
RWNCSA-AC-003.description: Ensure users operate with least privilege; administrative access restricted to authorized personnel

pass_conditions: Regular users are not in admin group

steps: Remove unnecessary admin: sudo dseditgroup -o edit -d username -t user admin

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Privilege escalation, unauthorized access

RWNCSA-AC-004.name: Remote Access - SSH Configuration
RWNCSA-AC-004.description: Secure SSH configuration with strong authentication and limited access

steps: Disable SSH if not needed: sudo systemsetup -setremotelogin off
- Disable root: echo 'PermitRootLogin no' | sudo tee -a /etc/ssh/sshd_config
- Key-only auth: echo 'PasswordAuthentication no' | sudo tee -a /etc/ssh/sshd_config
- Restart SSH: sudo launchctl unload /System/Library/LaunchDaemons/ssh.plist && sudo launchctl load -w /System/Library/LaunchDaemons/ssh.plist

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: CRITICAL - Remote unauthorized access, brute force attacks

RWNCSA-AC-005.name: File and Directory Permissions
RWNCSA-AC-005.description: Sensitive system files and directories must have restrictive permissions

pass_conditions: Sensitive files are not group/world readable

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Unauthorized file modification, privilege escalation

RWNCSA-AC-006.name: Sudo Configuration Security
RWNCSA-AC-006.description: Sudo access must be restricted and properly audited

pass_conditions: Sudo access limited to authorized users
NOPASSWD not used for sensitive commands

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Privilege escalation, unauthorized admin access
risk_assessment.business_impact: Full system control by unauthorized users

RWNCSA-AC-007.name: Screen Lock Enforcement
RWNCSA-AC-007.description: Automatic screen lock must be enabled to prevent unauthorized physical access

macos_implementation.audit_command: defaults read com.apple.screensaver askForPassword && defaults read com.apple.screensaver idleTime

steps: Enable password on wake: defaults write com.apple.screensaver askForPassword -int 1
Set 15-min timeout: defaults write com.apple.screensaver idleTime -int 900

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Unauthorized physical access to unattended workstation

RWNCSA-AU-001.name: Audit System Configuration
RWNCSA-AU-001.description: macOS audit system must be properly configured and operational

steps: Check audit: sudo launchctl list | grep auditd

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - No audit trail, cannot detect or investigate incidents
risk_assessment.business_impact: Inability to perform forensics, compliance violation

RWNCSA-AU-003.name: Time Synchronization
RWNCSA-AU-003.description: System time must be synchronized with authoritative time source for accurate audit trails

steps: Set time server: sudo systemsetup -setnetworktimeserver time.apple.com

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Inaccurate audit timestamps, forensic challenges

RWNCSA-SY-001.name: Application Firewall
RWNCSA-SY-001.description: macOS Application Firewall must be enabled and properly configured

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: CRITICAL - Network-based attacks, unauthorized connections
risk_assessment.business_impact: Malware infection, data exfiltration, network compromise

RWNCSA-SY-002.name: FileVault Disk Encryption
RWNCSA-SY-002.description: Full disk encryption must be enabled to protect data at rest

fail_conditions: Encryption in progress but not complete

steps: Follow prompts to create recovery key
Check progress: fdesetup status (wait for completion)

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: CRITICAL - Data theft from stolen/lost devices, physical access

RWNCSA-SY-003.name: Bluetooth Security
RWNCSA-SY-003.description: Bluetooth must be disabled when not in use or properly secured

steps: Disable Bluetooth: sudo defaults write /Library/Preferences/com.apple.Bluetooth ControllerPowerState -int 0
- Disable 'Allow Bluetooth devices to wake this computer'

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Bluetooth attacks, unauthorized connections

RWNCSA-SY-004.name: Network Configuration Security
RWNCSA-SY-004.description: Network interfaces must be properly configured and secured

steps: Check for promiscuous: ifconfig -a | grep PROMISC
Disable IPv6 if not used: networksetup -setv6off Wi-Fi

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Network sniffing, unauthorized access

RWNCSA-SY-005.name: Anti-Malware Protection
RWNCSA-SY-005.description: XProtect and MRT (Malware Removal Tool) must be up-to-date

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Malware infection, zero-day vulnerabilities

RWNCSA-SY-006.name: File Sharing Services
RWNCSA-SY-006.description: File sharing services must be disabled unless explicitly required

steps: Check sharing: sudo launchctl list | grep -E 'smbd|AppleFileServer'
Disable via System Preferences → Sharing → uncheck File Sharing
Verify: sudo launchctl list | grep smbd

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Unauthorized file access, data exfiltration

RWNCSA-CO-001.name: Software Inventory
RWNCSA-CO-001.description: Maintain inventory of installed software for license and security management

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Unauthorized software, license violations

RWNCSA-CO-003.name: Patch Management
RWNCSA-CO-003.description: Security updates and patches must be applied in timely manner

macos_implementation.expected_output_pattern: Software Update found|No new software available

remediation.expected_result: No new software available (or only non-critical)

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: CRITICAL - Exploitation of known vulnerabilities
risk_assessment.business_impact: System compromise, malware infection, data breach

RWNCSA-SY-007.name: Wi-Fi Security Configuration
RWNCSA-SY-007.description: Wireless network connections must use strong encryption and authentication

pass_conditions: All Wi-Fi networks use WPA2 or WPA3

steps: Disable auto-join: networksetup -setairportpower en0 off (when not needed)
Configure WPA2/WPA3: System Preferences → Network → Wi-Fi → Advanced

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Man-in-the-middle attacks, traffic interception

RWNCSA-SY-008.name: VPN Configuration and Usage
RWNCSA-SY-008.description: VPN must be used for remote connections and properly configured

pass_conditions: Split tunneling disabled for corporate VPN
fail_conditions: VPN not enforced for remote access

steps: Configure VPN: System Preferences → Network → + → VPN
Select IKEv2 or IPSec for strong encryption
Disable split tunneling in VPN settings
remediation.expected_result: VPN service configured with secure protocol

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Unencrypted remote access, data exposure

RWNCSA-SY-009.name: DNS Security Configuration
RWNCSA-SY-009.description: DNS must be configured securely to prevent spoofing and malicious redirection

pass_conditions: DNS servers are trusted (corporate or reputable public)
No suspicious DNS servers (known malicious IPs)

steps: Enable DoH: Configure in DNS preferences or via profile

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - DNS spoofing, malware redirection

RWNCSA-ID-002.name: Biometric Authentication Configuration
RWNCSA-ID-002.description: Touch ID/Face ID must be properly configured for enhanced security

steps: Enable Touch ID: System Preferences → Touch ID
Enable for sudo: sudo pam_touchid enable (if available)

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Weaker authentication, password-only access

RWNCSA-CO-004.name: Configuration Profiles Management
RWNCSA-CO-004.description: Configuration profiles must be documented and authorized

macos_implementation.expected_output_pattern: Configuration profiles|There are no configuration profiles

steps: Review each profile: profiles show -type configuration

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Unauthorized configuration changes, backdoors

RWNCSA-AC-008.name: Remote Desktop and Screen Sharing Security
RWNCSA-AC-008.description: Remote desktop and screen sharing must be disabled unless explicitly required and secured

pass_conditions: Screen Sharing disabled (unless justified and secured)

steps: Check services: sudo launchctl list | grep -E 'screensharing|RemoteDesktop'
Disable Screen Sharing: System Preferences → Sharing → uncheck Screen Sharing
Disable Remote Management: System Preferences → Sharing → uncheck Remote Management
If needed, enable with: VNC password, user authentication, logging
Verify: sudo launchctl list | grep screensharing
remediation.expected_result: No output (services not running) or secured configuration

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Unauthorized remote access, screen hijacking
risk_assessment.business_impact: Data theft, system control by attacker

RWNCSA-AC-009.name: Multi-Factor Authentication Status
RWNCSA-AC-009.description: Multi-factor authentication must be configured for privileged accounts

pass_conditions: Smart card or certificate authentication available

steps: Configure smart card: System Preferences → Security & Privacy → Smart Cards
Enable certificate authentication for admin accounts

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Single-factor authentication vulnerable to credential theft

RWNCSA-AC-011.name: Account Lockout Policy
RWNCSA-AC-011.description: Account lockout policy must be enforced after failed login attempts

pass_conditions: Failed attempt counter resets after 15 minutes

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Brute force attacks can succeed with unlimited attempts

RWNCSA-AC-012.name: Guest Account Status
RWNCSA-AC-012.description: Guest account must be disabled to prevent unauthorized access

pass_conditions: Guest account does not exist or is disabled
Guest login disabled in System Preferences
fail_conditions: Guest home folder exists and is accessible

steps: Disable guest account: System Preferences → Users & Groups → Guest User → uncheck

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Unauthorized users can access system without credentials

RWNCSA-AC-013.name: Automatic Login Disabled
RWNCSA-AC-013.description: Automatic login must be disabled to require authentication at startup

pass_conditions: Login window requires username and password
fail_conditions: System boots directly to user desktop

steps: Disable auto-login: System Preferences → Users & Groups → Login Options → Automatic login: Off
Or via command: sudo defaults delete /Library/Preferences/com.apple.loginwindow autoLoginUser

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Physical access = immediate system access without authentication
risk_assessment.business_impact: Unauthorized access, data theft from physical access

RWNCSA-AC-014.name: Fast User Switching Configuration
RWNCSA-AC-014.description: Fast user switching should be configured according to organizational policy

steps: Configure: System Preferences → Users & Groups → Login Options → Show fast user switching menu
Document organizational policy on multiple concurrent sessions

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: LOW - Minor session management issue

RWNCSA-AC-015.name: Password Reset Requirements
RWNCSA-AC-015.description: Password reset process must be secured and require proper authentication

pass_conditions: User verification required for self-service reset
fail_conditions: Password can be reset without proper authentication
No logging of password reset events

steps: Ensure administrator approval required for resets
Verify: Check security logs for password change events

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Unauthorized password resets can lead to account takeover
risk_assessment.business_impact: Account compromise via password reset abuse

RWNCSA-AC-016.name: Inactive Account Detection
RWNCSA-AC-016.description: Inactive user accounts must be detected and disabled after 90 days

pass_conditions: Last login tracked for all accounts
Accounts inactive > 90 days flagged for review
Process exists to disable inactive accounts
fail_conditions: No tracking of last login dates
No review process for inactive accounts

macos_implementation.audit_command: dscl . -list /Users | while read user; do echo "$user: $(dscl . -read /Users/$user lastlogin 2>&1 | grep -v 'No such key')"; done

steps: List users and last login: dscl . -list /Users | while read user; do dscl . -read /Users/$user lastlogin; done
Disable inactive accounts: sudo dscl . -append /Users/username AuthenticationAuthority ;DisabledUser;
Document review process for inactive accounts
remediation.expected_result: All accounts active within 90 days or documented as inactive

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Orphaned accounts can be exploited for unauthorized access

RWNCSA-AC-017.name: Root Account Status
RWNCSA-AC-017.description: Root account must be disabled or secured with strong authentication

pass_conditions: Sudo is used for privileged operations
fail_conditions: Root password is set and can be used for login

steps: Disable root: System Preferences → Users & Groups → Login Options → Network Account Server: Join → Directory Utility → Edit → Disable Root User
Or via command: sudo dscl . -create /Users/root UserShell /usr/bin/false
remediation.expected_result: Root account disabled or no authentication authority set

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: CRITICAL - Enabled root account with password = full system compromise risk
risk_assessment.business_impact: Complete system control, data theft, malware installation

RWNCSA-AC-018.name: User Home Directory Permissions
RWNCSA-AC-018.description: User home directories must have proper permissions to prevent unauthorized access

pass_conditions: Home directories owned by respective users

remediation.expected_result: All home directories with drwx------ (700) permissions

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Other users can read private files, credentials, sensitive data
risk_assessment.business_impact: Data exposure, credential theft, privacy violation

RWNCSA-AC-019.name: Shared Folder Permissions
RWNCSA-AC-019.description: Shared folders must have appropriate permissions based on access requirements

pass_conditions: Access to shared folders is logged

steps: Remove unauthorized shares: sudo sharing -r sharename
remediation.expected_result: Only authorized shares present with appropriate permissions

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Unauthorized file access, data leakage via shared folders

RWNCSA-AC-020.name: Login Banner Configuration
RWNCSA-AC-020.description: Login banner must be displayed warning unauthorized users

pass_conditions: Banner contains authorized use only warning

steps: Add text: 'AUTHORIZED USE ONLY. Unauthorized access prohibited and will be prosecuted.'
Configure PolicyBanner: System Preferences → Security & Privacy → General → Show message when screen is locked
remediation.expected_result: Banner present with authorized use warning

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: LOW - Legal implications if unauthorized access occurs
risk_assessment.business_impact: Reduced legal standing in prosecution of unauthorized access

RWNCSA-AC-021.name: Login Grace Time Configuration
RWNCSA-AC-021.description: Login grace time must be minimized to prevent credential guessing

pass_conditions: Login grace time is set to 0 (immediate password required)
fail_conditions: Screen saver allows grace period without authentication

steps: Check grace time: defaults read /Library/Preferences/com.apple.screensaver askForPasswordDelay
Or via GUI: System Preferences → Security & Privacy → General → Require password immediately

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Grace period allows brief unauthorized access to unlocked system

RWNCSA-AC-022.name: System Preferences Access Control
RWNCSA-AC-022.description: Access to System Preferences must be controlled and logged

pass_conditions: System Preferences changes require administrator authentication
Non-admin users cannot modify system settings
fail_conditions: Standard users can modify system settings
No authentication required for system changes

steps: Check authorization: security authorizationdb read system.preferences
If not, configure: sudo security authorizationdb write system.preferences
Enable audit logging for preference changes
remediation.expected_result: authenticate-admin required for system preference changes

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Users can modify system security settings without authorization

RWNCSA-AC-023.name: Keychain Access Control
RWNCSA-AC-023.description: System and user keychains must be properly secured

pass_conditions: Login keychain synchronized with user password
fail_conditions: System keychain accessible to standard users

steps: Check keychain settings: Keychain Access → Preferences
Enable: Lock after X minutes of inactivity (recommended: 5 minutes)
Ensure login keychain password matches user password
remediation.expected_result: Keychains configured with proper security settings

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Stored credentials accessible, password theft possible
risk_assessment.business_impact: Credential theft, unauthorized access to saved passwords

RWNCSA-AC-024.name: Terminal Access Restrictions
RWNCSA-AC-024.description: Terminal and command-line access should be restricted to authorized users

pass_conditions: Terminal access restricted to authorized users
Standard users have appropriate shell access
fail_conditions: All users have unrestricted terminal access

steps: Review which users need terminal access
Disable shell for service accounts: sudo dscl . -create /Users/serviceaccount UserShell /usr/bin/false
Enable terminal logging: sudo log config --mode 'level:debug,persist:debug'
remediation.expected_result: Appropriate shells configured per user role

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Unnecessary command-line access increases attack surface

RWNCSA-AC-025.name: SSH Key Authentication
RWNCSA-AC-025.description: SSH key authentication must be properly configured and managed

pass_conditions: SSH keys are used instead of passwords (where applicable)
Private key permissions are 600 or more restrictive
SSH keys are documented and reviewed regularly
fail_conditions: Weak or outdated SSH keys in use

steps: Remove weak keys and regenerate: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C 'user@host'
remediation.expected_result: SSH keys present with secure permissions (600)

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: HIGH - Compromised SSH keys lead to unauthorized remote access
risk_assessment.business_impact: Unauthorized remote access, lateral movement in network

RWNCSA-AC-026.name: Network Access Control (Packet Filter)
RWNCSA-AC-026.description: Packet filter (pf) rules must be configured to control network access

pass_conditions: Rules are documented and reviewed regularly

steps: Check pf status: sudo pfctl -s info
Document all allow rules and their justification
remediation.expected_result: Packet filter enabled with appropriate rules

risk_assessment.if_non_compliant: MEDIUM - Network traffic not controlled, unnecessary ports exposed
